summary.narrative: First African-American and longest-serving Buffalo mayor. 2021 write-in victory against progressive challenger. Focus on neighborhood development, affordable housing, diversity, and gun violence reduction. Resigned October 2024.
summary.grade_rationale: Demonstrated measurable crime reductions (28-58% gun violence decline 2022-2023) and business diversity initiatives. Limited transparency on platform specificity; incomplete survey responses. Long tenure but mixed legacy on housing affordability expansion.

promises.normalized: Reduce gun violence and shooting incidents
Increase neighborhood development across all areas
Support minority and women-owned business enterprises

data_gaps: Campaign platform details unavailable; Ballotpedia survey not completed
Specific metrics for neighborhood development and affordable housing targets missing
Buffalo Opportunity Agenda Phase Two outcomes and implementation timeline unclear
Executive order details on gun violence office structure and budget not specified
State funding advocacy results and impact quantification absent
Housing affordability metrics and units created/preserved not documented in sources
